name: Greek entity crius
shortDescription: Crius was one of the twelve Titan children of Uranus (Sky) and Gaia (Earth). He represents a personification of constellations and cosmic harmony, playing a role in the early cosmos.
description: Crius, also spelled Krios, was one of the second-generation Titans in Greek mythology, born to Uranus (Sky) and Gaia (Earth). He was one of the twelve Titans who preceded the Olympian gods. Crius's role is not as extensively detailed as some of his siblings like Cronus or Oceanus, leaving him somewhat obscure in surviving myths. He is primarily known through genealogical accounts. He married Eurybia, a daughter of Pontus (Sea) and Gaia, and together they had three sons: Astraeus (god of dusk and stars), Pallas (god of warfare), and Perses (god of destruction). While the specific domains directly associated with Crius are not explicitly stated in primary sources, his lineage and connections suggest an association with celestial phenomena, constellations, and potentially cosmic harmony. He is often interpreted as a pillar of the cosmos, representing a force maintaining the balance of the universe. Crius's importance lies primarily in his role as a link between the primordial deities and later generations of gods and Titans, and in his contribution to the Titan lineage.
